The visa fee is an administration fee which covers the cost of processing your application. This fee cannot be refunded if your application is refused or withdrawn.
Visa Fee Exemptions Some applicants are exempt from the requirement to pay the visa fee. Check below if the visa fee does not apply to you.
Nationalities exempt from visa fees

[irl > tur > en > Table:Conference > Visa Fees 2]
Family members of Irish Citizens who are exempt from visa fees (on production of the required documents) 
- Spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse with Irish Nationality)
- Separated spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse and their Irish Nationality) - subject to judicial separation
- Widow/widower (subject to death certificate)
- Child (under 18 years)
- Adopted child (under 18 years - subject to adoption papers)
Family member of EEA citizen who is exempt from visa fees
Spouse registered as a student or in employment (subject to submission of marriage certificate, passport of EEA citizen and, if applicable, details of educational institution)
Beneficiaries of Directive 2004/38/EC (Free Movement Directive) i.e. ‘qualifying family members’ of EU/EEA /Swiss Citizens are exempt from visa fees.
- Spouse
- Child ( under 21 years)
- Child (under 21 years) of the spouse
- Adopted child (subject to adoption papers)
- Dependent parent
- Dependent parent of the spouse
- Other dependent family members in the direct ascending line ( e.g. grandparent) or descending line (e.g. grandchild)
- Other dependent family members of the spouse in the direct ascending (e.g. grandparent) or descending line ( e.g. grandchild)

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
You can apply for this visa if you want to travel to Ireland for a conference, relating to your work, studies, or for an event. On this page you will find useful guidelines and details
                                                                        of requirements for Conference Visa application.
An applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category. 

## WHO SHOULD APPLY?
All Turkish passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
If you are not a Turkish passport holder, please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Pages/Countries%20whose%20nationals%20are%20visa%20required)
Please amend the following paragraph to read:
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Visa Waiver Programme (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6inYbF77poWCBsbQJ18LCz/09884ff8fb495f0c1c97d33c02362467/Information-Note-waiver-programme.pdf), may be
                                                                        able to travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K.
Regarding visa applications for all Libyan Nationals please click here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/our-services/visas/visas-for-ireland/).

## Visa Fees

[irl > tur > en > Table:Conference > Visa Fees 1]
The visa fee is an administration fee which covers the cost of processing your application. This fee cannot be refunded if your application is refused or withdrawn.
Visa Fee Exemptions Some applicants are exempt from the requirement to pay the visa fee. Check below if the visa fee does not apply to you.
Nationalities exempt from visa fees

[irl > tur > en > Table:Conference > Visa Fees 2]
Family members of Irish Citizens who are exempt from visa fees (on production of the required documents) 
- Spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse with Irish Nationality)
- Separated spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse and their Irish Nationality) - subject to judicial separation
- Widow/widower (subject to death certificate)
- Child (under 18 years)
- Adopted child (under 18 years - subject to adoption papers)
Family member of EEA citizen who is exempt from visa fees
Spouse registered as a student or in employment (subject to submission of marriage certificate, passport of EEA citizen and, if applicable, details of educational institution)
Beneficiaries of Directive 2004/38/EC (Free Movement Directive) i.e. ‘qualifying family members’ of EU/EEA /Swiss Citizens are exempt from visa fees.
- Spouse
- Child ( under 21 years)
- Child (under 21 years) of the spouse
- Adopted child (subject to adoption papers)
- Dependent parent
- Dependent parent of the spouse
- Other dependent family members in the direct ascending line ( e.g. grandparent) or descending line (e.g. grandchild)
- Other dependent family members of the spouse in the direct ascending (e.g. grandparent) or descending line ( e.g. grandchild)
Other persons who are exempt from visa fees
- Holders of diplomatic passports
- Programme Refugees (within the meaning of the Refugee Act 1996)
All references to 'spouse' also apply to a civil partner i.e. a person who has contracted a registered partnership, or is a party to a class of legal relationship specified in an order
                                                                        under section 5 of the Civil Partnership and Certain Rights and Obligations of Cohabitants Act 2010 as entitled to be recognized as a civil partnership.
Please  Note: 
- There is a service charge of 40 Euro (Inclusive of 20% VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees
- The visa application centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[irl > tur > en > Table:Conference > Visa Fees 3]
- Please click here for more information on the optional additional services.
- The Visa Fee has to be paid in Eurosin cash.
Note: 
All fees and service charge listed above are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash, please note that change will be given in Euro.

## Documents Required
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation
                                                                        for the omission. 
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
Conference checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1JNckzZLtiWz3La6HGUnPo/a8a8ff9dfb15e6a9dd25044fc01f7346/conference.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size 
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance 
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                            It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Applicants may apply up to three months before their intended travel dates. You may reach the most recent processing times from the Irish Department of Foreign Affair’s website : https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/ (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/)

## Online form and questionnaire
The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click on the link: www.visas.inis.gov.ie (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx)
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant office
                                                                        as indicated by the on-line system. 
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form. 
Information on completing online application is available to download here in English . (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6ox6NAkdgEEK6w57QpgA16/806c5108d388485769d5835436e75108/VAF_leaflet_in_english.pdf)
Information on completing online application is available to download here in Turkish (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3aogazFpw7mS4143iRLV18/3625a05ed015b0881de4a52a64479317/VAF_leaflet_in_turkish.pdf).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to come to Ireland to reside for longer than 3 months with a family member who is an Irish citizen or who is lawfully resident in Ireland you can apply for a long
                                                                                stay (join family) visa. 
A comprehensive statement of national immigration policy on the area of family reunification is contained in the Policy Document on Non-EEA  Family Reunification (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Family%20Reunification%20Policy%20Document.pdf/Files/Family%20Reunification%20Policy%20Document.pdf) published on 31 December 2013.
 The policies outlined in the document apply to all decision making in relation to family reunification cases where ministerial discretion applies including long stay (join family)
                                                                                visa applications. 
The policies do not apply to: 
- applications pursuant to Directive 2504/38/EC on the rights of citizens of the Union and their family members to move and reside freely within the territory of Member States
- applications for family reunification by recognised refugees or persons granted subsidiary protection.
Before  you apply for a long stay (join family) visa please read the Policy Document  carefully to find out:
- who is eligible to be a sponsor for the purposes of an application for family reunification,
- who may be sponsored and the eligibility requirements,
- whether waiting periods apply before a person becomes eligible to sponsor,
- whether a sponsor must achieve a minimum level of earnings prior to being eligible to sponsor,
- what a person who is granted a join family visa may do in Ireland,
- how dependency is measured when this is adduced in support of an application for family reunification.

Overview
You can apply for this visa if you are working outside Ireland and want to come to Ireland to carry out activities relating to your job, such as to attend meetings, attend trade shows
                                                                        for promotional work, arrange deals or negotiate or sign trade agreements or contracts, or carry out fact finding missions. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls
                                                                        under this category.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
You can apply for this visa if you are working outside Ireland and want to come to Ireland to carry out activities relating to your job, such as to attend meetings, attend trade shows
                                                                        for promotional work, arrange deals or negotiate or sign trade agreements or contracts, or carry out fact finding missions. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls
                                                                        under this category. 

## WHO SHOULD APPLY?
All Turkish passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
If you are not a Turkish passport holder, please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Pages/Countries whose nationals are visa required)
Please amend the following paragraph to read:
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Visa Waiver Programme (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6inYbF77poWCBsbQJ18LCz/09884ff8fb495f0c1c97d33c02362467/Information-Note-waiver-programme.pdf), may be
                                                                        able to travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K. 
Regarding visa applications for all Libyan Nationals please click here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/our-services/visas/visas-for-ireland/).

## Visa Fees

[irl > tur > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]
The visa fee is an administration fee which covers the cost of processing your application. This fee cannot be refunded if your application is refused or withdrawn.
Visa Fee Exemptions Some applicants are exempt from the requirement to pay the visa fee. Check below if the visa fee does not apply to you.
Nationalities exempt from visa fees

[irl > tur > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 2]
Family members of Irish Citizens who are exempt from visa fees (on production of the required documents) 
- Spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse with Irish Nationality)
- Separated spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse and their Irish Nationality) - subject to judicial separation
- Widow/widower (subject to death certificate)
- Child (under 18 years)
- Adopted child (under 18 years - subject to adoption papers)
Family member of EEA citizen who is exempt from visa fees
Spouse registered as a student or in employment (subject to submission of marriage certificate, passport of EEA citizen and, if applicable, details of educational institution)
Beneficiaries of Directive 2004/38/EC (Free Movement Directive) i.e. ‘qualifying family members’ of EU/EEA /Swiss Citizens are exempt from visa fees.
- Spouse
- Child ( under 21 years)
- Child (under 21 years) of the spouse
- Adopted child (subject to adoption papers)
- Dependent parent
- Dependent parent of the spouse
- Other dependent family members in the direct ascending line ( e.g. grandparent) or descending line (e.g. grandchild)
- Other dependent family members of the spouse in the direct ascending (e.g. grandparent) or descending line ( e.g. grandchild)
Other persons who are exempt from visa fees
- Holders of diplomatic passports
- Programme Refugees (within the meaning of the Refugee Act 1996)
All references to 'spouse' also apply to a civil partner i.e. a person who has contracted a registered partnership, or is a party to a class of legal relationship specified in an order
                                                                        under section 5 of the Civil Partnership and Certain Rights and Obligations of Cohabitants Act 2010 as entitled to be recognized as a civil partnership.
Please  Note: 
- There is a service charge of 40 Euro (Inclusive of 20% VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees
- The visa application centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[irl > tur > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 3]
- Please click here for more information on the optional additional services.
- The Visa Fee has to be paid in Eurosin cash.
Note: 
All fees and service charge listed above are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash, please note that change will be given in Euro.

## Documents Required
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation
                                                                        for the omission. 
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
Business Visa Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1uPaGG9UsC9Jd55JvTvA7r/28c7fd67cf7824b5c9f0879ba234c9cd/business__3_.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size 
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance 
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                            It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Applicants may apply up to three months before their intended travel dates. You may reach the most recent processing times from the Irish Department of Foreign Affair’s website : https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/ (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/)

## Online form and questionnaire
The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click on the link: www.visas.inis.gov.ie (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx)
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant office
                                                                        as indicated by the on-line system. 
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form. 
Information on completing online application is available to download here in English . (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6ox6NAkdgEEK6w57QpgA16/806c5108d388485769d5835436e75108/VAF_leaflet_in_english.pdf)
Information on completing online application is available to download here in Turkish (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3aogazFpw7mS4143iRLV18/3625a05ed015b0881de4a52a64479317/VAF_leaflet_in_turkish.pdf).

## Overview
The Embassy of Ireland in Turkey has introduced a new scheme for Turkish students who wish to attend short English language courses in Ireland. This visa category is for Turkish
                                                                                junior students (aged 12-18). Under this programme, provided all the requirements are met. 
Further information on this visa scheme is available in this document (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/Zk7Ro3r4jjZpjeE3UxP7O/e09da29b1f860e961eb0587617abc537/tuition_for_Turkish_Juniors.pdf) . 
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.

## WHO SHOULD APPLY?
All Turkish passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
If you are not a Turkish passport holder, please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Pages/Countries%20whose%20nationals%20are%20visa%20required)
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Visa Waiver Programme, (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6inYbF77poWCBsbQJ18LCz/09884ff8fb495f0c1c97d33c02362467/Information-Note-waiver-programme.pdf) may be able to
                                                                                travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K.
Regarding visa applications for all Libyan Nationals please click here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/our-services/visas/visas-for-ireland/).
Please note: Student visas cannot be placed into Turkish Diplomatic Passports. If you hold a Diplomatic Passport, you must apply for a Ordinary or Special Passport prior to making
                                                                                your application.

## Visa Fees

[irl > tur > en > Table:Visas For Turkish Junior Students To Study English > Visa Fees 1]
The visa fee is an administration fee which covers the cost of processing your application. This fee cannot be refunded if your application is refused or withdrawn.
Visa Fee Exemptions Some applicants are exempt from the requirement to pay the visa fee. Check below if the visa fee does not apply to you.
Nationalities exempt from visa fees

[irl > tur > en > Table:Visas For Turkish Junior Students To Study English > Visa Fees 2]
Family members of Irish Citizens who are exempt from visa fees (on production of the required documents) 
- Spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse with Irish Nationality)
- Separated spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse and their Irish Nationality) - subject to judicial separation
- Widow/widower (subject to death certificate)
- Child (under 18 years)
- Adopted child (under 18 years - subject to adoption papers)
Family member of EEA citizen who is exempt from visa fees
Spouse registered as a student or in employment (subject to submission of marriage certificate, passport of EEA citizen and, if applicable, details of educational institution)
Beneficiaries of Directive 2004/38/EC (Free Movement Directive) i.e. ‘qualifying family members’ of EU/EEA /Swiss Citizens are exempt from visa fees.
- Spouse
- Child ( under 21 years)
- Child (under 21 years) of the spouse
- Adopted child (subject to adoption papers)
- Dependent parent
- Dependent parent of the spouse
- Other dependent family members in the direct ascending line ( e.g. grandparent) or descending line (e.g. grandchild)
- Other dependent family members of the spouse in the direct ascending (e.g. grandparent) or descending line ( e.g. grandchild)
Other persons who are exempt from visa fees
- Holders of diplomatic passports
- Programme Refugees (within the meaning of the Refugee Act 1996)
All references to 'spouse' also apply to a civil partner i.e. a person who has contracted a registered partnership, or is a party to a class of legal relationship specified in
                                                                                an order under section 5 of the Civil Partnership and Certain Rights and Obligations of Cohabitants Act 2010 as entitled to be recognized as a civil partnership.
Please  Note: 
- There is a service charge of 40 Euro (Inclusive of 20% VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees
- The visa application centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[irl > tur > en > Table:Visas For Turkish Junior Students To Study English > Visa Fees 3]
- Please click here for more information on the optional additional services.
- The Visa Fee has to be paid in Eurosin cash.
Note: 
All fees and service charge listed above are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash, please note that change will be given in Euro.

## Documents Required
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation
                                                                                for the omission. 
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
English Language tuition Visa checklist for Turkish Juniors (aged 12- 18) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/feaqoT5NnRLXyLg6NyhEf/87900dc4b3adaa35f9534e2d92347b79/meiforjuniors.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size 
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance 
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                                    It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Applicants may apply up to three months before their intended travel dates. You may reach the most recent processing times from the Irish Department of Foreign Affair’s website : https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/ (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/)

The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click on the link: www.visas.inis.gov.ie (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx)
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant
                                                                                office as indicated by the on-line system. 
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form. 
Information on completing online application is available to download here in English . (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6ox6NAkdgEEK6w57QpgA16/806c5108d388485769d5835436e75108/VAF_leaflet_in_english.pdf)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Short  Term Scientific Research
This visa category is intended to allow a foreign national to visit Ireland to conduct scientific research. 
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.

                                                             Scientific Researcher – Hosting Agreement
You can apply for an Employment (Scientific Researcher) Visa if you are seeking to come to Ireland for the purposes of carrying out research under a “hosting agreement”.
You are advised to familiarise yourself with the immigration arrangements applying to scientific researchers seeking to come to Ireland under a hosting agreement before you make your
                                                                        visa application.
If your immediate family members intend to join you in Ireland, and are also visa required, each member must make a separate application for a visa. Guidelines on making Join Family visa
                                                                        applications can be found here.
You can apply for an Employment (Scientific Researcher) visa up to 3 months before your date of travel to Ireland.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The Embassy of Ireland in Turkey has introduced a new Programme for English Language tuition for Turkish Third Level Students, Recent University Graduates and Executive Level
                                                                                Employees. 
Further information on this visa scheme is available in this document (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/48u6IgOaqVFCUuG3inzEf7/37f8c6098c56e6d40c20307d0c53187a/University_Graduates.pdf) .
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
You  can apply for a study visa up to 3 months before your date of travel to Ireland.

## WHO SHOULD APPLY?
All Turkish passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
If you are not a Turkish passport holder, please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Pages/Countries%20whose%20nationals%20are%20visa%20required)
Citizens  of one of seventeen countries covered by the scheme, may be able to travel to  Ireland if they have a valid U.K. General C Visa and meet Visa Waiver Programme  requirements.
Regarding visa applications for all Libyan Nationals please click here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/our-services/visas/visas-for-ireland/).

## Visa Fees

[irl > tur > en > Table:Visas For Turkish Third Level/university Students To Study English > Visa Fees 1]
The visa fee is an administration fee which covers the cost of processing your application. This fee cannot be refunded if your application is refused or withdrawn.
Visa Fee Exemptions Some applicants are exempt from the requirement to pay the visa fee. Check below if the visa fee does not apply to you.
Nationalities exempt from visa fees

[irl > tur > en > Table:Visas For Turkish Third Level/university Students To Study English > Visa Fees 2]
Family members of Irish Citizens who are exempt from visa fees (on production of the required documents) 
- Spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse with Irish Nationality)
- Separated spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse and their Irish Nationality) - subject to judicial separation
- Widow/widower (subject to death certificate)
- Child (under 18 years)
- Adopted child (under 18 years - subject to adoption papers)
Family member of EEA citizen who is exempt from visa fees
Spouse registered as a student or in employment (subject to submission of marriage certificate, passport of EEA citizen and, if applicable, details of educational institution)
Beneficiaries of Directive 2004/38/EC (Free Movement Directive) i.e. ‘qualifying family members’ of EU/EEA /Swiss Citizens are exempt from visa fees.
- Spouse
- Child ( under 21 years)
- Child (under 21 years) of the spouse
- Adopted child (subject to adoption papers)
- Dependent parent
- Dependent parent of the spouse
- Other dependent family members in the direct ascending line ( e.g. grandparent) or descending line (e.g. grandchild)
- Other dependent family members of the spouse in the direct ascending (e.g. grandparent) or descending line ( e.g. grandchild)
Other persons who are exempt from visa fees
- Holders of diplomatic passports
- Programme Refugees (within the meaning of the Refugee Act 1996)
All references to 'spouse' also apply to a civil partner i.e. a person who has contracted a registered partnership, or is a party to a class of legal relationship specified in
                                                                                an order under section 5 of the Civil Partnership and Certain Rights and Obligations of Cohabitants Act 2010 as entitled to be recognized as a civil partnership.
Please  Note: 
- There is a service charge of 40 Euro (Inclusive of 20% VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees
- The visa application centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[irl > tur > en > Table:Visas For Turkish Third Level/university Students To Study English > Visa Fees 3]
- Please click here for more information on the optional additional services.
- The Visa Fee has to be paid in Eurosin cash.
Note: 
All fees and service charge listed above are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash, please note that change will be given in Euro.

## Documents Required
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation
                                                                                for the omission. 
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
English Language tuition for Turkish Third Level Students, Recent University Graduates and Executive Level Employees visas Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2O82DUfVOHHAqFQHNURelK/c52f7b79b95f8fd4769b11d68c6fe7c1/mei.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size 
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance 
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                                    It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Applicants may apply up to three months before their intended travel dates. You may reach the most recent processing times from the Irish Department of Foreign Affair’s website : https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/ (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/)

## Online form and questionnaire
The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click on the link: www.visas.inis.gov.ie (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx)
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant
                                                                                office as indicated by the on-line system. 
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form. 
Information on completing online application is available to download here in English . (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6ox6NAkdgEEK6w57QpgA16/806c5108d388485769d5835436e75108/VAF_leaflet_in_english.pdf)
Information on completing online application is available to download here in Turkish (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3aogazFpw7mS4143iRLV18/3625a05ed015b0881de4a52a64479317/VAF_leaflet_in_turkish.pdf).
